title: Filter 'Bump to' commits from generated tag-message body
Vocabulary spec: unify source/kind naming across -C, timing, indicators
description:
The current scheme is source-first (`installed:f`, `local:c`, `taps`).
User flagged "keep forgetting which order" as a real ergonomic problem.

Several places need to agree on one spelling:
- `-C` cache-status rows (current: `installed:f`)
- Timing footer labels (draft spec: `installed:f`, `refresh:installed:f`)
- Installed-indicator column (draft spec just uses ●/○)
- `--refresh=KIND` selector (current: `installed`, short `i`)
- Table column headers (current: `S` = single letter)

Candidates:
A. Keep source-first long form everywhere: `installed:formula`,
   `local:cask`, `taps`. Most readable, longest.
B. Compact two-letter (`if`, `lc`, `t`) — short but cryptic.
C. Type-first reversal: `f:installed`, `c:local` — reads "formula
   from installed", aligns with how -f/-c are kind filters today.
D. Single canonical long, single canonical short, both documented:
   `installed:formula` ↔ `i:f`; aliases freely.

Write a spec doc at docs/specs/drafts/cli-vocabulary.md.

Driver: timing.md "Open questions" + installed-indicator.md cross-refs.

title: Scoped verbosity sub-args: -v=t, -v=tt, -v=ttcl style
description:
User proposal: instead of a single linear -v/-vv/-vvv ladder,
accept letter-tagged increments where each character bumps one
dimension's verbosity.

Examples:
  -v=t       # +1 timing detail
  -v=tt      # +2 timing detail
  -v=ttcl    # +2 timing, +1 cache, +1 labels
  -v=c       # default everything else, just more cache detail

Bare -v / -vv keeps current behavior (linear ladder); the kv form
is for users who want to dial in one axis without dragging the
others up.

Dimensions to consider:
- t : timing footer (default 1 line; tt = per-source; ttt = per-step)
- c : cache section (default minimal; c = per-table; cc = with TTL/ETA)
- l : labels (default short kind:f; l = installed:formula full)
- o : op/operation trace (debug-tier, exposes the timer context)

Risk: argparse doesn't natively support this; needs a custom action.
Risk: collides with --verbose=N alias.

Driver: timing.md "Open questions" §3.

Defer pending: vocabulary spec landing (the letter set has to map
to canonical dimension names).

title: Stale-result hash for diff detection in cache-flow trailing line
description:
docs/specs/features/cache-flow.md promises a trailing line variant
"↻ updated, results may differ — re-run". Realizing this requires
the foreground process to remember what it printed, then re-run the
same FTS query against the post-refresh cache and diff.

Two implementations on the table:
A. Snapshot the printed `(name, version)` tuples in memory; rerun
   query, compare set equality.
B. Hash the result envelope (e.g. sha256 over a canonical JSON
   serialization). Cheaper memory, harder to debug.

Either way the data has to live until the bg refresh's sentinel
fires (which is bounded by the cache-flow grace window: 2s default).

Open: do we store this between invocations (so a second `bhs python`
N seconds later can show the diff against the previously-printed
result set)? Probably no — scope creep.

Driver: cache-flow.md "Differs/matches detection" section + the
2026-05-28 grace-window change.
